text.en: Hatred is never ended by hatred. Hatred ends only through love. This is an ancient and unchanging truth.
Through effort, attentiveness, and self-restraint, the wise build within themselves an island that no flood can ever wash away.
The mind is restless and hard to restrain, rushing wherever it wishes. To gentle it is good, for a settled mind brings peace.
Do not dwell on the faults of others, on what they have done or failed to do. Look instead to your own actions, to what you have done and left undone.
"These are mine, this wealth is mine" — such thoughts torment the unwise. You do not even belong to yourself; how then can sons or wealth?
As a great rock stands unmoved by the wind, the wise are not shaken by blame or by praise.
For the one who has completed the journey, released sorrow, and let go of every bond, there is no more suffering.
Though one may conquer a thousand times a thousand in battle, the greatest victor is the one who conquers himself.
Do not take small wrongs lightly, thinking they will not reach you. Drop by drop a pot is filled; little by little, harm gathers within.
Do not take small goodness lightly, thinking it will not reach you. Drop by drop a pot is filled; little by little, the wise become full of good.
Those who channel water guide it where they will; the arrow-maker shapes the arrow; the carpenter shapes the wood. In the same way, the good shape themselves.
How can there be laughter, how can there be joy, when the world is forever burning? Surrounded by darkness, why do you not seek the light?
You are your own refuge; who else could be? When you have truly mastered yourself, you find a refuge rare and hard to gain.
By yourself wrong is done; by yourself you suffer. By yourself wrong is left undone; by yourself you are made pure. Purity and impurity are your own; no one can purify another.
